text:
🧠 Entropy: One of the Most Important Concepts in AI, Machine Learning, Information Theory, and Physics

If you understand entropy, you'll better understand:

• Decision Trees
• Cross Entropy Loss
• LLM Training
• Compression Algorithms
• Diffusion Models
• Statistical Physics
• Information Theory

Entropy is everywhere.

---

What is Entropy?

Entropy measures uncertainty, randomness, or information content.

The more unpredictable something is, the higher its entropy.

The more predictable something is, the lower its entropy.

Think of entropy as:

"How surprised would I be by the next outcome?"

---

Example 1: Coin Toss

Fair Coin:

Heads = 50%
Tails = 50%

You cannot predict the outcome.
Maximum uncertainty.
High entropy.

---

Biased Coin:

Heads = 99%
Tails = 1%

You can almost always predict Heads.

Low uncertainty.
Low entropy.

---

Example 2: Language Models

Consider predicting the next word:

"The sun rises in the ____"

Possible next token:

• east → 95%
• west → 3%
• sky → 2%

Very predictable.

Low entropy.

---

Now:

"Today I dreamed about a ____"

Possible next token:

• dragon
• robot
• elephant
• spaceship
• castle

Many possibilities.

Higher uncertainty.
Higher entropy.

---

Claude Shannon's Insight

In 1948, Claude Shannon introduced information entropy.

The formula:

Entropy = Expected Information Content

A highly predictable event carries little information.
A surprising event carries a lot of information.

---

Why Compression Works

Suppose a file contains:
AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA

Very repetitive.

Low entropy.
Easy to compress.

---

Suppose a file contains:
X7$P@9L#2W!K8Z&Q

Looks random.

High entropy.
Difficult to compress.

Compression is essentially exploiting low entropy.

---

Entropy in Decision Trees

Algorithms like:

• ID3
• C4.5
• Random Forests

Use entropy to determine the best split.

Good splits:

Reduce uncertainty.

Bad splits:

Leave uncertainty unchanged.

The objective:

Maximum Information Gain.

---

Entropy in Deep Learning

Modern neural networks use:

Cross Entropy Loss

For:

• Classification
• Language Models
• Transformers
• LLMs

Cross entropy measures:

How different are model predictions from reality?

Lower cross entropy means better predictions.

---

Entropy in LLM Training

During pretraining:

Model
↓
Predict Next Token
↓
Compute Cross Entropy Loss
↓
Update Weights
↓
Reduce Prediction Error

Every GPT-style model optimizes cross entropy billions of times during training.

---

Entropy and Temperature

LLMs use temperature during generation.

Low Temperature:

• Lower entropy
• More deterministic
• More predictable

High Temperature:

• Higher entropy
• More creative
• More diverse

Temperature directly affects uncertainty in generated text.

---

Entropy in Reinforcement Learning

Modern RL algorithms often maximize entropy.

Why?

Without entropy:

Agent repeats the same actions.

With entropy:

Agent explores new possibilities.

Exploration requires uncertainty.

---

Entropy in Diffusion Models

Diffusion models work by:

Image
↓
Add Noise
↓
Increase Entropy
↓
Random Distribution

Generation:

Random Distribution
↓
Remove Noise
↓
Decrease Entropy
↓
Image

The entire process can be viewed as moving between low and high entropy states.

---

Entropy in Physics

In thermodynamics:
Entropy measures disorder.

Low entropy:
Ice cube.
Highly structured.

---

High entropy:

Steam.
Highly disordered.

The second law of thermodynamics states:
Entropy tends to increase over time.

The company that taught machines to imagine just built a machine to see inside your body.

A full body medical scanner.

You step into a pool of warm water. Half a million sensors pulse ultrasonic waves through your body from every angle.

Sixty seconds. No radiation. No magnets. No claustrophobia.

A 3D map of your organs down to a fraction of a millimeter. A hundred times the speed of an MRI. A few dollars instead of thousands.

They mastered text-to-image. Now they’re doing sound-to-biology.

Midjourney isn’t putting this in a hospital. They’re building a spa.

Saunas. Cold plunges. Rooms of golden light that quietly scan your biology while you relax.

The scan is a side effect of showing up.

First location opens in San Francisco, late 2027. By 2031 they’re targeting 50,000 scanners worldwide. A billion scans per month.

Fewer than twelve of these machines at full speed could outperform every MRI on Earth combined.

Zero investors. Zero venture capital. Just subscription revenue from everyday people paying to generate AI art.

A community funded research lab just walked into the most protected industry on the planet.

We mapped the ocean floor. Photographed black holes. Sequenced the entire human genome.

And the only way to see inside your own body was to be sick enough or rich enough to justify the scan.

This inverts that.

Monthly scans. AI tracking your organs over time. Anomalies flagged years before a single symptom appears.

You stop reacting to disease and start watching for it.

At this scale, preventive imaging could avert 30% of all deaths and cut healthcare spending in half. Debate the numbers. The logic underneath is airtight.

What breaks your brain is who built it. Not a pharmaceutical giant. Not a government lab. Nine people at an AI art company.

The team that spent years teaching machines to generate things that don’t exist just built one that reveals the thing that always did.

You have lived your entire life inside a body you have never truly seen.

You’re about to.

hermes v0.17.0 just made every paid agent tool look silly

iMessage native,whatsapp official,background workers,image editing

and this is totally free.

nous research just dropped the biggest hermes agent release yet.

v0.17.0 "the reach release" 800+ PRs, 245 contributors, 1,475 commits, 198k github stars.

one release that does more than most paid tools do in a year.

imessage. no mac required.
> run: hermes photon login
> done. your agent can text anyone with an iphone.
> no bluebubbles. no relay. no hack.

whatsapp. official. no bridge.
> whatsapp business cloud api native support
> not some qr-code bridge that breaks every month

background subagents. no babysitting.
> delegate_task(background=true)
> spawn a research agent, walk away, it reports back later
> you keep working. the agent works for you.

image editing. with words.
> image_generate now edits existing images
> "make this logo blue" done
> "remove the background" done
> "turn this sketch into a render" done
> no photoshop. no design skills. just describe it.

automation blueprints. no syntax.
> "daily news briefing at 8am" generates the schedule
> no cron, no yaml, no terminal wizardry

everything else in v0.17.0:
> full profile builder in browser never touch config.yaml
> redesigned skills hub with security scans
> rebindable shortcuts + native notifications
> live subagent watch windows
> vs code themes in the desktop app
> atomic batch memory operations
> grok composer 2.5 support

how to get it there:

step 1: terminal
> hermes update

step 2: pick your weapon
> hermes photon login own your imessage
> try a background agent delegate_task(background=true)
> or just chat everything works out of the box

bookmark this before the next release.

and follow @rakib_web3 for more alpha like this.

The robotics world model simulator wars are heating up. 

WEAVER is a robot manipulation world model that's fast, accurate, AND consistent.

- improves the π0.5 robot foundation model's real-world success rate by 38% with zero real-world interaction, evaluates policies at 0.87 correlation with reality, and plans 5-10x faster than the prior best

- multi-view transformer predicts future latents and rewards via flow matching, borrowing JEPA-style latent prediction, diffusion forcing for long horizons, a pretrained encoder for robustness, and a reward head so it can score actions without calling a slow external VLM judge.

- why it matters: Synthetic rollouts from WEAVER train policies as well as real data (within 4%), and can evaluate policy success rate with a best-in-class MMRV
